book_claims.claim: Consuming whole flaxseed results in significant reduction in total and LDL-cholesterol
book_claims.mechanism: Richest plant source of antioxidant lignans (up to 13mg/g) and omega-3 alpha-linolenic acid. Takes <2 weeks for omega-3 to incorporate into cell membranes. Enterolignans (produced by gut bacteria from lignans) associated with lower heart disease and cancer risk.

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis (Pan et al., 2009; updated Khalesi et al., 2015) of 28 RCTs found ground flaxseed consumption significantly reduced total cholesterol (by 0.10 mmol/L), LDL cholesterol (by 0.08 mmol/L), and blood pressure, with strongest effects in whole/ground flaxseed versus flaxseed oil.

FlaxPAD trial (Rodriguez-Leyva et al., 2013) -- a double-blind RCT -- found 30g ground flaxseed daily for 6 months reduced systolic BP by 10 mmHg and diastolic by 7 mmHg in hypertensive patients, one of the strongest dietary BP-lowering effects ever measured.

Systematic review (DeLuca et al., 2018) found flaxseed lignan metabolites (enterolactone, enterodiol) inversely associated with breast cancer risk, with highest enterolignan producers showing 20-30% lower risk in prospective studies.

culinary_pairings.source: Classic porridge addition; complementary soluble fibres
Lignans + anthocyanins for combined antioxidant and anticancer pathways
Acid preserves ALA from oxidation; brightens earthy flavor
Combined plant omega-3 sources; salad topping

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Richest common food source of plant omega-3. Must GRIND before eating -- whole seeds pass through undigested. ALA incorporates into cell membranes within 2 weeks. Conversion to EPA ~5-8%, DHA ~0.5-5%.
Richest food source of lignans by 100x over most other foods. Gut bacteria convert SDG to enterolactone and enterodiol, which have anti-estrogenic and anticancer properties.
Mucilage forms gel in water, slowing glucose absorption and feeding beneficial gut bacteria. Also contributes to the laxative effect.
